And whereas, copies of the said Gazette were made available to the public on the 26fi day ofOctober 2007; And whereas, the objection received in respect of the said draft Order was duly considered by the Central Govemment; Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by sub'section (2) of section 27 read with section 28 of the Insecticides Act, 1968 (46 of 1968), the Central Govemment hereby makes the following Order, namely:- ORDER I . This Order may.be called the Restriction on Use of Dazomet Order, 2008. 2 . Use of Dazomet is not permitted on tea- 3. This Order may be called the Banning of ChlorofenvinphoS Order, 2008. 2. And whereas tle Central Governmen! after considering the recommendations of the said Expert Group and after consultation with the Registration Committee, set up under the said Act is satisfied that the use of Iprodione involves health hazards to human beings and environment; The Draft Order, which the Central Government proposes to make, is hereby published for information of all persons likely to be affected thereby; and notice is hereby given that the said Draft Order shall be taken into consideration after expiry ofa period of forty-five days from the date on which the copies of the Gazette of India containing the Order are made available to the public. Any suggestion or objection in respect of the said Draft Order may be forwarded to the Central Govemment within the period so specified to the Joint Secreta-ry (Plant Protection), Ministry of Agriculture, Department of Agriculture and Cooperation, Krishi Bhavan, New Delhi-l 10 001. Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (2) of section 27 read with section 28 of the Insecticides Act, 1968 (46 of 1968), the Central Government hereby proposes to make the following Order, namely: DRAFT ORDER l. this"Ordermay be called-the.Ranning.oflpmdione Order,200t^ 2. No oerson shall use Iorodione in Asriculture. 3. The Registration Committee shall sall back the certificates of registration granted for lprodione ftom all regishants including new registrants. 4. If any person who holds the certificate of registration fails to retum the certificate to the Registation Committee, referred to in clause (3), within a period of six months, actioir shall be taken under section 14 ofthe said Act. 5. bvery State Govemment shall take all such steps under the provisions of the said Act and the rules made thereunder, as it considers neessary for the ' execution of this Order in the Stare. E E E E E F E V T -- 7 of 8 -- E TTIE GA.ZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY IPARr I[---SEC. 3(ii)1 s.o. 3009(E).-Whereas, the Central Govemment has constinrted the Registration committee under section 5 of ttre lnsecticides Act. 1968 (46 of 196g) and set up an Expert Group with a view to investigate as to whether the continued ue of certain imecticides in India will involve such risk to human beings or -animals as it render it expedient or necessary to take immediate action; And whereas the Central Govemmen! after considering the recommendations of the said Expert Group and after consultation with the Registration committee set up under the said Act is satisfied that the use of Linuron involves health hazards to human beings and environmentl The Draft Order, which the Central Covemment proposes to make, is hereby published for infonnation ofall persons likely to be affected thereby; and notice is hereby given that the said Draft order shall be taken into consideration after expiry'ofa period of forty-five days from the date on which the copies of the Gazette of India containing the Order are made available to the public. Any suggestion or objection in respect ofthe said Draft Order may be forwared to the Central Government within the period so specified to the Joint Se'cretary (plant Protection), Ministry of Agriculnre, Department of Agriculture and cooperation, Krishi Bhavan, New Delhi- l I 0 001 . Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (2) of section 27 read with section 28 of the Insecticides Act" 1968 (46 of 1968), the Cental Govemment hereby proposes to make the following Order, namely: NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 3l st December, 2008 DRAFT ORDER 1. This Order may be called the Banning of Linuron Order, 2008. 2. No person shall use Linuron in Agriculture. 3. The Registration Committee shatl call back the certificates of registration grarted furl-imtron f,orr@ 4. If any person who holds the certificat€ of regis&ation fails to retum the certificate to the Registation CoEmifi€e, referred to in clause (3), witbin a period of six months, action shall be taken under Section 14 of the said Acf 5. Every State Govemment shall take all such steps under the provisions of the said Act and the nrles made thereunder, as it considers necessary for the execution of this Order in the State. [F.
